title: Embrace Faith & Fresh Start
subtitle: Day 0–1
description: Congratulations on taking this beautiful step! This stage is about celebrating your decision to embrace Islam and taking the very first steps of your new journey. There is no rush — simply absorb the moment and begin with what feels right.
mainGoal: Declare your faith through the Shahada and perform the purification bath (Ghusl) to mark your fresh start.
success: You have spoken the Shahada with sincerity and performed Ghusl. You feel a sense of peace and new beginning.
dontWorry: Don't worry about memorizing long prayers right now — that comes later.
Don't worry about changing your name — it is not required in Islam.
Don't worry about knowing everything — learning is a lifelong journey.
Don't worry about what others think — this is between you and God.

title: Building Foundations
subtitle: Week 1
description: Now that you have embraced Islam, it is time to build your foundations. This week focuses on the most essential daily practice — prayer — and connecting with your local Muslim community. Take it one day at a time.
mainGoal: Learn the basics of the five daily prayers and visit a local masjid to start building community.
success: You are attempting some daily prayers (even if imperfectly) and have visited a masjid or connected with other Muslims.
dontWorry: Don't worry about perfect Arabic pronunciation in prayer.
Don't worry if you miss a prayer — just try to catch the next one.
Don't worry about fitting in at the masjid — everyone was new once.
Don't worry about knowing all the prayer movements yet.

title: Strengthening Practice
subtitle: Weeks 2–4
description: You are settling into your new routine. This stage deepens your connection with the Quran and strengthens the habits you have started building. Consistency matters more than perfection.
mainGoal: Begin reading or listening to the Quran regularly and establish a consistent daily routine.
success: You have a basic Quran reading routine and your daily prayers are becoming more natural.
dontWorry: Don't worry about reading Arabic fluently — translations are perfectly fine.
Don't worry about understanding every verse — focus on what resonates.
Don't worry if you struggle with consistency — building habits takes time.

title: Broadening Practice
subtitle: Months 2–3
description: By now, you have a foundation in prayer and Quran. This stage broadens your understanding of Islamic lifestyle — from dietary guidelines to the spiritual practice of fasting. These are practical aspects that integrate faith into daily life.
mainGoal: Understand halal and haram basics and learn about fasting so you are prepared when the time comes.
success: You understand basic dietary guidelines and have knowledge of how fasting works. You may have tried a voluntary fast.
dontWorry: Don't worry about overhauling your entire pantry overnight.
Don't worry if fasting feels hard at first — it gets easier.
Don't worry about every ingredient label — start with the obvious items.
Don't worry about social situations — you will find your rhythm.

title: Deepening Faith
subtitle: Months 4–6
description: Your practice is becoming more natural. This stage focuses on inner growth — developing noble character traits that Islam emphasizes and deepening your knowledge of the faith. This is where Islam starts transforming not just what you do, but who you are.
mainGoal: Focus on developing Islamic character (akhlaq) and continue learning about your faith through study and community.
success: You notice positive changes in your character and daily interactions. You feel more confident in your knowledge of Islam.
dontWorry: Don't worry about being a perfect Muslim — growth is gradual.
Don't worry about scholarly-level knowledge — focus on what's practical.
Don't worry about moments of doubt — they are natural and can strengthen faith.

title: Lifelong Growth
subtitle: Beyond 6 Months
description: You have come so far! Islam is a lifelong journey of learning and growing closer to Allah. This stage introduces pillars you will grow into over time — charitable giving (Zakat) and the pilgrimage (Hajj). These are longer-term goals, not immediate obligations for most new Muslims.
mainGoal: Understand Zakat and Hajj as future milestones and continue your lifelong journey of growth and learning.
success: You understand the remaining pillars of Islam and feel equipped to continue learning independently. From here, keep practicing and explore the Topics section for deeper learning.
dontWorry: Don't worry about Hajj — it is a once-in-a-lifetime obligation when you are able.
Don't worry about Zakat if you do not meet the financial threshold.
Don't worry about knowing everything — even lifelong Muslims are always learning.
